entity_extraction_template: Composite key columns: '_col_1' → '公共支出' → '私人支出' → '总计' → '_col_6' → '_col_7' → '_col_8' → '_col_9' → '_col_10' → '_col_11' → '_col_12' → '_col_13' → '_col_14' → '_col_15' → '_col_16' → '_col_17' → '_col_18' → '_col_19'. Empty cells inherit the last non-empty value from the same column above. Top-level key '_col_1' → Col entity. Second-level key '公共支出' → 公共支出 entity. Leaf key '_col_19' → 私人支出 entity.
relation_extraction_template: Create HAS_SUB_ITEM relations between hierarchy levels: (Col) -[HAS_SUB_ITEM]-> (公共支出). Create HAS_VALUE relations from leaf entities to numeric columns: . Attach remarks columns ['_col_5', '_col_0'] as metadata properties on the leaf entity.
extraction_constraints: Detect hierarchy by sparse fill: empty cells in key columns inherit the parent value.
Top-level key '_col_1' defines the root entity (Col).
Second-level key '公共支出' defines child entities (公共支出) linked to their parent via HAS_SUB_ITEM.
Leaf key '_col_19' defines the most granular entity (私人支出).
Numeric columns become HAS_VALUE relations from the leaf entity.
Remarks/notes columns become metadata properties (not separate entities).
Skip rows that are entirely empty or contain only section headers.
